response.justification: Candidate has 15+ years of software development experience with strong full-stack, web, and some embedded exposure, but the profile is a poor match for this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency (not listed), strong C programming for UNIX with Linux kernel modification experience (only brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments ~2014-2015), and embedded networking security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, switch chips) — none of which appear on the resume. Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang for web and data platforms, which does not align with embedded LAN-switch firmware development.
strengths: Long track record (15+ years) across diverse domains including some embedded and low-latency systems work
Strong software craftsmanship discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and open-source contributions
Some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and Linux familiarity from infrastructure and GIS platform work
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency listed (hard requirement) — only English, Romanian, and basic German
No demonstrated Linux kernel investigation/modification experience or recent professional C development for UNIX
No embedded networking security background — missing MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, NETCONF/SNMP, switch chip SDKs

key_factors.factor: No embedded LAN-switch / networking security domain experience
Limited recent C/UNIX and Linux kernel modification experience
